papers.title: Light as Deception: GPT-driven Natural Relighting Against Vision-Language Pre-training Models
papers.abstract:
While adversarial attacks on vision-and-language pretraining (VLP) models have been explored, generating natural adversarial samples crafted through realistic and semantically meaningful perturbations remains an open challenge. Existing methods, primarily designed for classification tasks, struggle when adapted to VLP models due to their restricted optimization spaces, leading to ineffective attacks or unnatural artifacts. 
To address this, we propose **LightD**, a novel framework that generates natural adversarial samples for VLP models via semantically guided relighting. Specifically, LightD leverages ChatGPT to propose context-aware initial lighting parameters and integrates a pretrained relighting model (IC-light) to enable diverse lighting adjustments. LightD expands the optimization space while ensuring perturbations align with scene semantics. Additionally, gradient-based optimization is applied to the reference lighting image to further enhance attack effectiveness while maintaining visual naturalness.
The effectiveness and superiority of the proposed LightD have been demonstrated across various VLP models in tasks such as image captioning and visual question answering.

papers.title: MP-Nav: Enhancing Data Poisoning Attacks against Multimodal Learning
papers.abstract: Despite the success of current multimodal learning at scale, its susceptibility to data poisoning attacks poses security concerns in critical applications. Attacker can manipulate model behavior by injecting maliciously crafted yet minute instances into the training set, stealthily mismatching distinct concepts. Recent studies have manifested the vulnerability by poisoning multimodal tasks such as Text-Image Retrieval (TIR) and Visual Question Answering (VQA). However, the current attacking method only rely on random choice of concepts for misassociation and random instance selections for injecting the poisoning noise, which often achieves the suboptimal effect and even risks failure due to the dilution of poisons by the large number of benign instances. This study introduces MP-Nav (Multimodal Poison Navigator), a plug-and-play module designed to evaluate and even enhance data poisoning attacks against multimodal models. MP-Nav operates at both the concept and instance levels, identifying semantically similar concept pairs and selecting robust instances to maximize the attack efficacy. The experiments corroborate MP-Nav can significantly improve the efficacy of state-of-the-art data poisoning attacks such as AtoB and ShadowCast in multimodal tasks, and maintain model utility across diverse datasets. Notably, this study underscores the vulnerabilities of multimodal models and calls for the counterpart defenses.

queries.query_text: adversarial attacks on vision language models that edit visual semantics such as objects and scenes to produce incorrect outputs
methods for generating semantic adversarial examples against multimodal large language models by manipulating image content
non pixel attacks that alter human interpretable visual features to fool vision language pretraining models
semantic level visual attacks that cause hallucination or misclassification in multimodal models
adversarial attacks that manipulate semantic visual content such as objects, attributes, or scenes in images to fool vision-language models
adversarial examples created by modifying visual semantics to mislead multimodal large language models
attacks targeting vision-language models that exploit human-interpretable visual modifications
inducing visual hallucinations in multimodal models by modifying image content semantically
semantic-level perturbations to images for attacking vision-language models

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Proposes LightD, generating natural adversarial samples for VLP models via semantically guided relighting
llm_ranked.evidence_cn: 提出LightD，通过语义引导的重新打光为视觉语言预训练模型生成自然对抗样本
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: 提出LightD，通过语义引导的重新打光为视觉语言预训练模型生成自然对抗样本
llm_ranked.tldr_en: LightD leverages ChatGPT to suggest context-aware lighting parameters and a pre-trained relighting model to create realistic adversarial images that effectively deceive vision-language pre-training models, achieving semantic-level adversarial attacks.
llm_ranked.tldr_cn: 针对现有方法难以生成自然且语义有意义的对抗样本的挑战，LightD利用ChatGPT提出上下文相关的初始光照参数，结合预训练重光照模型生成自然的对抗图像，成功攻击视觉语言预训练模型，证明了语义层视觉攻击的有效性。
llm_ranked.title_zh: 以光为欺：GPT驱动的自然重光照攻击视觉语言预训练模型
llm_ranked.motivation_cn: 现有VLP对抗攻击方法因优化空间受限，难以生成自然且语义显著的扰动。
llm_ranked.method_cn: LightD利用ChatGPT建议上下文感知的初始光照参数，结合IC-light重光照模型，通过语义引导的重新打光生成对抗样本。
llm_ranked.result_cn: 实验表明，LightD生成的对抗样本能有效欺骗VLP模型，且保持高度自然。
llm_ranked.conclusion_cn: 该工作首次通过语义引导的重新打光实现了针对VLP模型的高效自然对抗攻击，揭示了语义层视觉扰动的重要性。
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: semantic-level perturbations to images for attacking vision-language models

llm_ranked.evidence_en: Enhancing data poisoning attacks against multimodal learning by mismatching concepts
llm_ranked.evidence_cn: 增强针对多模态学习的数据投毒攻击，通过错误关联概念
llm_ranked.canonical_evidence: 增强针对多模态学习的数据投毒攻击，通过错误关联概念
llm_ranked.tldr_en: MP-Nav enhances data poisoning attacks on multimodal models for text-image retrieval and VQA by optimizing concept misassociation and instance selection, improving attack effectiveness.
llm_ranked.tldr_cn: 现有针对多模态学习的数据投毒攻击因随机选择概念和样本而效果欠佳。MP-Nav提出通过更优的导航策略来增强攻击，恶意注入少量样本以错误关联不同概念，从而在文本-图像检索和视觉问答任务中更好地操控模型行为。实验表明该方法提高了投毒攻击的成功率和隐蔽性，为多模态模型的安全性研究提供了新的攻击视角。
llm_ranked.title_zh: MP-Nav：增强针对多模态学习的数据投毒攻击
llm_ranked.motivation_cn: 现有多模态数据投毒攻击因随机选择概念和样本导致攻击效果不理想，需要增强攻击策略。
llm_ranked.method_cn: 提出MP-Nav方法，通过优化概念错误关联和有毒样本选择来增强投毒攻击。
llm_ranked.result_cn: 在文本-图像检索和VQA任务上，攻击成功率和隐蔽性得到提升。
llm_ranked.conclusion_cn: MP-Nav展示了更有效的多模态投毒攻击，突显了多模态学习安全风险。
llm_ranked.matched_query_text: Papers central to 搜索2025-2026年针对多模态模型的语义级视觉攻击论文。目标论文通过操纵人类可解释的视觉语义（如对象、属性、关系、场景、局部概念或细粒度图像内容）攻击VLMs、LVLMs或MLLMs。优先考虑非进化方法，生成或编辑语义视觉内容以误导模型输出，如字幕、VQA答案、识别、幻觉或视觉推理。排除主要基于OCR文本、印刷提示、隐藏指令、隐写术、代码截图、漫画、文本越狱、纯像素噪声、综述、基准或仅防御方法的论文。, especially work that connects or combines: adversarial attacks that manipulate semantic visual content such as objects, attributes, or scenes in images to fool vision-language models; attacks targeting vision-language models that exploit human-interpretable visual modifications; adversarial examples created by modifying visual semantics to mislead multimodal large language models; inducing visual hallucinations in multimodal models by modifying image content semantically; semantic-level perturbations to images for attacking vision-language models; adversarial attacks on vision language models that edit visual semantics such as objects and scenes to produce incorrect outputs; methods for generating semantic adversarial examples against multimodal large language models by manipulating image content; semantic level visual attacks that cause hallucination or misclassification in multimodal models; non pixel attacks that alter human interpretable visual features to fool vision language pretraining models.
